note: v0 seed. Items recast from the AI Research-Automation Scorecard (fully sourced). Tiers use the CANONICAL Source Incentive Map / working-tracker scale: 1 = least incentive to shade ... 5 = the party selling the thing the claim is about. FLAG, do not NARRATE.

code_automation.label: Labs' code-% claims: 0 of 8 define a denominator and none is externally audited. METR's randomised trial (Jul 2025) found developers 19% slower with frontier tools; a 2025 Science study puts AI-written US GitHub Python at ~29%.

work_automation.label: A 'work' claim, not a code claim: the broadest denominator in the set, spanning whole functions and tied to headcount reductions. No stated base for the percentage.

self_improvement.label: No lab reports its recursive-self-improvement red-line crossed. The one observed instance (AlphaEvolve) is a ~1% end-to-end training saving from a human-run tool, not an autonomous agent.

power_demand.label: Announced datacentre capacity is not delivered capacity. The ledger separates contingent, announced megawatts from robust, contracted demand; only a fraction of announced capacity reaches operation.
power_demand.source: Contingent vs Robust AI Power Demand

energy_forecast.label: Published AI energy-demand forecasts vary widely and rarely state their base assumptions. The scorecard grades projections on transparency and reproducibility rather than repeating the headline number.

cost.label: Headline AI spend often omits what it is a cost of and over what period. Cost Watch tracks the recurring gaps between announced spend and the underlying unit economics.

water.label: Datacentre water claims rarely state on-site versus off-site use or the basis year. The tracker reproduces a site-level water index and opens the hidden hydropower coupling.

items.headline: Anthropic: AI writes ~90% of our code, heading to essentially all of it
OpenAI: agentic tools went from 20% to 80% of your code (within December)
Microsoft: 20-30% of code in our repos is written by software
Google: from ~30% to ~75% of new code AI-generated and approved by engineers
Meta: maybe half of development done by AI next year (a target)
Salesforce: AI is doing 30-50% of the work here now
Amazon: 79% of AI code-review suggestions shipped without changes (Amazon Q)
Snap: AI writes more than two-thirds of our new code (credits Anthropic)
DeepMind: AlphaEvolve optimised a kernel used to train Gemini (~1% training saving)
